Tests that `Math.min()` returns `Infinity` when called with no arguments.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test script that uses an assertion to verify the behavior of the `Math.min()` function in JavaScript. The script checks that when `Math.min()` is called with no arguments, it returns `Infinity`, which is the expected behavior according to the ECMAScript specification. This functionality is narrow, focusing solely on validating this specific aspect of the `Math.min()` function. The use of an assertion indicates that this code is likely part of a test suite to ensure the correct implementation of JavaScript's mathematical functions.
